evals.prompt: Convert the PRD in plugins/project-advisor/skills/prd-to-issues/evals/inputs/prd-order-history.md into Jira-ready vertical-slice action items. Assume the slice breakdown is approved and write the markdown files directly.
evals.expected_output: Generated action items use outcome-oriented scenarios and avoid implementation-task wording such as file, endpoint, schema, component, or method changes.
expectations: At least one generated action item covers chronological order history with user-visible summary fields and a first-time empty state.
At least one generated action item covers invoice access together with auditable access and retryable recovery.
The generated action items describe desired outcomes and constraints rather than instructing developers to add specific endpoints, schemas, tables, components, or methods.

All ticket content — titles, user stories, scenario names, scenario text, note labels (Was umgesetzt werden soll, Blockiert durch, Technische Hinweise, Annahmen, Abhängigkeiten, Risiken, Offene Fragen), and note content — is written in German. No English labels like 'What to build', 'Blocked by', 'Technical notes', 'Assumptions', 'Dependencies', 'Risks', or 'Open questions' appear.
Note entries that carry no real information (e.g. 'Keine', 'Offen', 'Nicht vorhanden') are omitted entirely rather than included as placeholder entries.
Scenario text does not contain filler adjectives like nahtlos, robust, umfassend, zuverlässig, eindeutig, or sichergestellt.
Scenarios use first-person perspective (ich befinde mich, ich klicke, ich sehe) rather than third-person (der Nutzer, der Kunde befindet sich).
Technische Hinweise only contain non-obvious information — no tech stack, standard auth rules, or other things a senior dev would assume.

evals.prompt: Convert the PRD in plugins/project-advisor/skills/prd-to-issues/evals/inputs/prd-delegated-approvals.md into Jira-ready vertical-slice action items. Assume the slice breakdown is approved and write the markdown files directly.
evals.expected_output: Generated action items describe user-visible behavior, constraints, and verifiable outcomes instead of prescribing implementation steps across layers.
expectations: At least one generated action item covers delegation setup with eligibility checks and clear validation feedback.
At least one generated action item covers automatic delegated routing during the active window and automatic return to the original approver afterward.
The generated action items describe behavior, ownership, and audit outcomes without prescribing specific APIs, schemas, tables, services, or UI components to change.

All ticket content — titles, user stories, scenario names, scenario text, note labels (Was umgesetzt werden soll, Blockiert durch, Technische Hinweise, Annahmen, Abhängigkeiten, Risiken, Offene Fragen), and note content — is written in German. No English labels like 'What to build', 'Blocked by', 'Technical notes', 'Assumptions', 'Dependencies', 'Risks', or 'Open questions' appear.
Note entries that carry no real information (e.g. 'Keine', 'Offen', 'Nicht vorhanden') are omitted entirely rather than included as placeholder entries.
Scenario text does not contain filler adjectives like nahtlos, robust, umfassend, zuverlässig, eindeutig, or sichergestellt.
Scenarios use first-person perspective (ich befinde mich, ich klicke, ich sehe) rather than third-person (der Nutzer, der Kunde befindet sich).
Technische Hinweise only contain non-obvious information — no tech stack, standard auth rules, or other things a senior dev would assume.
